overview: A multi-tier leave management system with a React frontend web application communicating with two backend Go services: a leave-service handling requests/approvals and a policy-service managing HR configurations. Services expose REST APIs, store data independently, and the frontend provides role-based views for employees, managers, and HR administrators.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests with date range and reason
Managers must view pending leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests
Employees must view remaining leave balance by type (vacation, sick, personal)
HR administrators must set annual leave allowances for employees
All users must view upcoming approved leave in calendar format
System must enforce leave balance constraints before approval
System must support role-based access (employee, manager, HR admin)
All services must expose health check endpoints
Services must handle concurrent request submissions safely

components.name: leave-web-app
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend application for leave management
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Build a React single-page application in TypeScript using Create React App or Vite. Implement three main views: (1) Employee Dashboard showing leave balance by type, form to submit new requests with date picker and reason textarea, and list of own requests with status; (2) Manager Dashboard displaying pending requests from direct reports with approve/reject buttons and team calendar view; (3) HR Admin Dashboard with forms to set employee allowances and view all leave policies. Use React Router for navigation. Implement role-based rendering - detect user role from context and show appropriate dashboard. Integrate with leave-service API for request CRUD operations and policy-service for balance/allowance data. Use fetch or axios for HTTP calls. Include a calendar component (react-calendar or similar) showing approved leave dates. Style with CSS modules or styled-components. Serve on port 3000. Docker multi-stage build: node:18 for build, nginx:alpine to serve static files. Health endpoint returns 200 OK. Handle API errors gracefully with user-friendly messages. Store user context (role, employeeId) in React Context or state management library.

components.name: leave-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Manages leave requests and approvals
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /leave-requests:
    post:
      summary: Submit a leave request
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - employeeId
                - leaveType
                - startDate
                - endDate
                - reason
              properties:
                employeeId:
                  type: string
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [vacation, sick, personal]
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request or insufficient balance
    get:
      summary: List leave requests
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
  /leave-requests/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get leave request by ID
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Request not found
  /leave-requests/{id}/approve:
    post:
      summary: Approve a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                comments:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request approved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Not authorized to approve
        '404':
          description: Request not found
  /leave-requests/{id}/reject:
    post:
      summary: Reject a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                comments:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request rejected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Not authorized to reject
        '404':
          description: Request not found
  /calendar:
    get:
      summary: Get approved leave for calendar view
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: teamId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: startDate
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date
        - name: endDate
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Calendar events
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: object
                  properties:
                    employeeId:
                      type: string
                    employeeName:
                      type: string
                    startDate:
                      type: string
                      format: date
                    endDate:
                      type: string
                      format: date
                    leaveType:
                      type: string
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        employeeName:
          type: string
        managerId:
          type: string
        leaveType:
          type: string
          enum: [vacation, sick, personal]
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        reason:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
        managerComments:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go REST API using net/http on port 9090. Create LeaveRequest struct with fields: ID, EmployeeID, EmployeeName, ManagerID, LeaveType (vacation/sick/personal), StartDate, EndDate, Reason, Status (pending/approved/rejected), ManagerComments, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt. Use in-memory storage (map with mutex for thread safety) keyed by request ID (use UUID). Implement POST /leave-requests to create requests - validate dates, check leave type, set status to pending, generate ID and timestamps. Before creating, call policy-service GET /balances/{employeeId} to verify sufficient balance for the requested leave type and duration. Calculate business days between start and end dates. Return 400 if insufficient balance. Implement GET /leave-requests with query filters for employeeId, managerId (to find direct reports), and status. Implement GET /leave-requests/{id}. Implement POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve and /reject - verify managerId matches request's managerId, update status and managerComments, set updatedAt. Implement GET /calendar - filter approved requests by optional employeeId, teamId, and date range, return simplified objects for calendar rendering. Add /health endpoint returning JSON {"status":"ok"}. Use json encoding for request/response. Set appropriate Content-Type headers. Return proper HTTP status codes. Log all requests. Dockerfile: multi-stage with golang:1.21 for build, distroless or alpine for runtime, expose 9090, copy binary and run it.

components.name: policy-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Policy Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Manages leave policies and employee balances
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /balances/{employeeId}:
    get:
      summary: Get leave balances for an employee
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Employee leave balances
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  employeeId:
                    type: string
                  balances:
                    type: object
                    properties:
                      vacation:
                        type: number
                      sick:
                        type: number
                      personal:
                        type: number
        '404':
          description: Employee not found
  /allowances:
    post:
      summary: Set annual leave allowance for an employee
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - employeeId
                - leaveType
                - amount
              properties:
                employeeId:
                  type: string
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [vacation, sick, personal]
                amount:
                  type: number
                adminId:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Allowance set successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Allowance'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
    get:
      summary: List all allowances
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of allowances
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Allowance'
  /balances/{employeeId}/deduct:
    post:
      summary: Deduct leave balance (called by leave-service on approval)
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - leaveType
                - days
              properties:
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [vacation, sick, personal]
                days:
                  type: number
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Balance deducted
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  employeeId:
                    type: string
                  balances:
                    type: object
                    properties:
                      vacation:
                        type: number
                      sick:
                        type: number
                      personal:
                        type: number
        '400':
          description: Insufficient balance
components:
  schemas:
    Allowance:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        leaveType:
          type: string
          enum: [vacation, sick, personal]
        amount:
          type: number
        adminId:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go REST API using net/http on port 9090. Create EmployeeBalance struct with EmployeeID and map of leave types to remaining days (vacation, sick, personal). Create Allowance struct with ID, EmployeeID, LeaveType, Amount, AdminID, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt. Use in-memory storage with two maps: one for balances keyed by employeeId, one for allowances keyed by allowance ID. Use mutex for thread safety. Implement GET /balances/{employeeId} - return current balance for all leave types, return 404 if employee not found, initialize with zero balances if new. Implement POST /allowances - HR admin sets annual allowance, validate leaveType enum, generate ID and timestamps, store allowance record, update or initialize employee balance map with the amount. Implement GET /allowances with optional employeeId query filter. Implement POST /balances/{employeeId}/deduct - called by leave-service when approving requests, validate leaveType and days, check sufficient balance, deduct days from balance map, return 400 if insufficient, return updated balances on success. Add /health endpoint returning JSON {"status":"ok"}. Use json encoding for all request/response. Set Content-Type application/json. Return proper HTTP status codes. Log all operations. Dockerfile: multi-stage with golang:1.21 for build, distroless or alpine for runtime, expose 9090, copy binary and run it.
